commitDisciplineCheck.verdict: FIRST-INSTANCE PASS — +1 closeout-as-mission rule prevented the sprint-90/91 retro-split failure mode. Each feature mission produced exactly one commit tagged with its mission ID at or before the mission's completion marker.

stage1Message.summary: OODS Sprint-92 complete — Stage1 v1.5.0 projection_variants[] write-side activated end-to-end

capturedLearnings: Contract-then-activate sequencing reduced sprint-92 scope to a pure activation pass (s91-m02 landed storage stubs; s92 flipped on write paths with zero new bilateral negotiation)
The +1 closeout-as-mission rule worked on its first instance — m01-m04 each produced exactly one commit tagged with its mission ID, zero retro-splits needed

The e2e fixture-driven validation in m03 pre-emptively caught a latent Stage1 v1.2.0 external_component rejection that the feature-code path would have hit only after deploying. Pattern: schema-acceptance checks should run BEFORE feature code uses new fields.
capturedDecisions: Sprint-93 theming candidate: Stage1 v1.5.0 rollup consumer surfaces (identity_graph + capability_rollup + object_rollup) + capability view in playground (alternative: runtime bridge.json loading)

Counterparty coordination: Stage1 status_update sent confirming v1.5.0 write-side activation on both map.create and map.apply paths with commit SHAs + re-gate evidence (201/201 variants byte-identical)
